nativeRange.habitat: Marshes, swamps, pond margins, and slow-moving streams; emergent or fully submerged in shallow water.

summary: North American stem plant with serrated, whorled leaves that tend to develop striking orange-red coloration under high light, CO2, and iron-rich fertilization.
careNotes: Proserpinaca palustris is a stem plant in the Haloragaceae family, generally advised for background or midground placement in planted tanks. Submerged growth produces finely serrated, whorled leaves that have been reported to shift from green to coppery orange-red when grown under high light with consistent CO2 (around 15-25 mg/L) and regular iron supplementation. The popular 'Cuba' trade variant — originally collected on Isla de la Juventud and distributed by Tropica — is commonly considered the most reliable form for aquascaping. Sources disagree on baseline care difficulty: Tropica rates it Advanced (reflecting the high-light/high-CO2 demands needed for the prized red coloration), while retail care guides commonly describe it as easy to moderate when grown for green submerged growth. The plant is generally tolerant of a broad temperature window (roughly 20-28°C) and pH around 6.0-7.5, with soft to moderately hard water. Regular pruning has been reported to encourage branching and bushier growth. Sometimes sold under the trade names Mermaid Weed or Marsh Mermaid Weed.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Haloragaceae), placement (background), pH range (6.5-7.5), lighting (medium-high), and confirmed iron supplementation drives red coloration.

additional.name: Aquarium Roots
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (72-82°F = 22-28°C) and pH (6.0-7.5). Tertiary source used because Tropica product page lacked explicit water parameters; care-level rating disagreement between Tropica (Advanced) and retailers (Easy-Moderate) averaged to Intermediate.
